best_for: Any email triggered by a request path: enqueue (DB table, QStash, SQS) and send from a worker with retries — the user's request never waits on SMTP
avoid_when: Truly fire-and-forget internal notifications where loss is acceptable
tradeoffs: Survives provider blips and traffic spikes; adds a queue component and a worker to operate

best_for: Retry-safe email: an idempotency key per logical send (order_id+template) checked before dispatch, so retries and double-clicks can't double-send
tradeoffs: No duplicate receipts/resets after a retry storm; one uniqueness check per send to implement

option: Bounce and complaint webhooks wired to suppression
best_for: Every provider: consume bounce/complaint webhooks, hard-bounces and complaints go on a suppression list the send path always checks
avoid_when: Trusting the provider's internal suppression alone when you send via multiple providers/streams
tradeoffs: Protects sender reputation automatically; one webhook endpoint + one table (verify webhook signatures — same rule as Stripe, EC checks)

option: One-click unsubscribe headers on all non-transactional mail
best_for: List-Unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe-Post (RFC 8058) headers — mandatory for bulk senders to Gmail/Yahoo since 2024
avoid_when: Transactional mail (receipts, resets) — unsubscribe there breaks the product
tradeoffs: Required for inbox placement at scale; honor within 48h and sync the opt-out to every system that sends

option: Explicit consent + double opt-in for marketing
best_for: KVKK/GDPR-clean lists: consent recorded with timestamp/source, double opt-in for newsletters
avoid_when: Purchased lists or auto-opt-in checkboxes — reputation and legal poison
tradeoffs: Smaller but real list, dramatically better engagement metrics; adds one confirmation step to signup

best_for: Templates in the repo, changed via PR, previewed across major clients (Gmail/Outlook/Apple Mail) before ship
avoid_when: Editing production templates live in a provider dashboard
tradeoffs: Email regressions become reviewable diffs; preview tooling (React Email preview, Litmus) is the cost

best_for: Every HTML email carries a real text/plain part (not an empty one)
tradeoffs: Better spam-filter scoring and accessibility for free; most frameworks generate it — verify it's not blank

best_for: New domains/IPs ramp sending gradually over 2-6 weeks (provider warmup schedules) before full volume
avoid_when: Blasting full volume from a cold domain — instant spam-folder placement
tradeoffs: Reputation built deliberately; launch timelines must include the warmup window

best_for: Transactional from mail.product.com, marketing from news.product.com — subdomain isolation (pairs with EM007)
avoid_when: Sending everything from the root domain — mixes reputations and complicates DMARC
tradeoffs: Blast-radius isolation per stream; a few more DNS records to manage

best_for: Products with Turkish + English users: template-level locale selection from the user record, localized subject lines
avoid_when: Machine-translating one template inline at send time
tradeoffs: Meets users in their language (subject lines drive open rates); one more dimension in the template matrix
